definitions.text: Physically unobstructed, uncovered, etc. Able to have something pass through or along it.
examples: The ice has cleared and the channel is open again.

definitions.text: Able to have something pass through or along it.
examples: The ice has cleared and the channel is open again.

definitions.text: Not covered, sealed, etc.; having an opening or aperture showing what is inside.
examples: The jewellery box was lying open.
Don't just stand there with your mouth open!

definitions.text: To make or become physically unobstructed, uncovered, etc. To make or become accessible or clear for passage by moving from a shut position.
examples: Turn the doorknob to open the door.
The door opened all by itself.

definitions.text: To make or become accessible or clear for passage by moving from a shut position.
examples: Turn the doorknob to open the door.
The door opened all by itself.

definitions.text: To make or become clear by removal of objects and obstructions, so as to allow passage, access, or visibility.
examples: The ice melted away and the channel opened.

definitions.text: Open or unobstructed space; an exposed location.
examples: I can't believe you left the lawnmower out in the open when you knew it was going to rain this afternoon!
Wary of hunters, the fleeing deer kept well out of the open, dodging instead from thicket to thicket.

definitions.text: Public knowledge or scrutiny; full view.
examples: We have got to bring this company's corrupt business practices into the open.

definitions.text: A defect in an electrical circuit preventing current from flowing.
examples: The electrician found the open in the circuit after a few minutes of testing.
